rows.title: Repo ownership dispute: acme-checkout vs acme-payment-api
rows.description: Both acme-checkout (service 4) and acme-payment (service 5) claim ownership of the acme-payment-api repository. This contradicts ADR-001 (one-team-one-repo policy) and creates ambiguity in the deployment graph. The conflict was discovered when acme-checkout CI tried to release acme-payment-api and was rejected by the deployment gate.

rows.title: API contract violation: acme-payment-v3 declares gRPC, ADR-006 mandates REST/JSON
rows.description: api-contract-payment-v3 was generated for acme-payment service 5 using gRPC + Protocol Buffers, but ADR-006 (canonical service interface policy) mandates REST/JSON for all synchronous service-to-service traffic. gRPC is allowed only for streaming-internal use cases (ADR-009). The contract was promoted to v3 without steward approval during the Q1 2027 roadmap push. This is a high-severity conflict because it breaks the canonical interface contract for the entire payment domain.

sources.rationale: ADR-006 mandates REST/JSON for sync traffic
ADR-009 restricts gRPC to streaming internal

rows.title: Deployment strategy mismatch: acme-frontend uses canary, ADR-007 mandates blue/green
rows.description: acme-frontend (service 1) deployment pipeline still uses Argo Rollouts canary strategy from a 2025 experiment. ADR-007 (deployment strategy standardization, ratified 2026-Q2) mandates blue/green for all customer-facing services. The migration was deprioritized after the canary experiment succeeded. Low severity because canary is functionally safe, but it is a standards violation that the steward must resolve before the 2027 audit cycle.

sources.rationale: ADR-007 mandates blue/green for customer-facing services
